---
title: "Defining Template"
description: "Create Go templates with method chaining and common builder operations."
---

# Defining Template

In the Go SDK, template definitions are built by chaining methods on `*e2b.TemplateBase`.

## Method chaining

All builder methods return the same template instance, so you can define a template fluently.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		FromUbuntuImage("22.04").
		AptInstall([]string{"curl"}, nil).
		SetWorkdir("/app").
		Copy("package.json", "/app/package.json", nil).
		RunCmd("npm install").
		SetStartCmd("npm start", e2b.WaitForPort(3000))

	_ = template
}
```

## User and workdir

Use `SetWorkdir(...)` and `SetUser(...)` to change where later steps run and which user they run as.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		SetWorkdir("/app").
		SetUser("node").
		SetUser("1000:1000")

	_ = template
}
```

For the full behavior of persisted defaults, see [User and Workdir](/docs/template/user-and-workdir).

## Copy files

Use `Copy(...)` for one or many sources, and `CopyItems(...)` when you want to describe several copy operations at once.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		Copy("package.json", "/app/package.json", nil).
		Copy([]string{"file1", "file2"}, "/app/file", nil).
		CopyItems([]e2b.CopyItem{
			{Src: "src/", Dest: "/app/src/"},
			{Src: "package.json", Dest: "/app/package.json"},
		}).
		Copy("config.json", "/app/config.json", &struct {
			User string
			Mode int
		}{
			User: "appuser",
			Mode: 0o644,
		})

	_ = template
}
```

`Copy(...)` source paths must stay inside `TemplateOptions.FileContextPath`.

## File operations

The builder also exposes helpers for remove, rename, directory creation, and symlinks.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		Remove("/tmp/temp-file.txt", nil).
		Remove("/old-directory", &struct{ Recursive bool }{Recursive: true}).
		Remove("/file.txt", &struct{ Force bool }{Force: true}).
		Rename("/old-name.txt", "/new-name.txt", nil).
		Rename("/old-dir", "/new-dir", &struct{ Force bool }{Force: true}).
		MakeDir("/app/logs", nil).
		MakeDir("/app/data", &struct{ Mode int }{Mode: 0o755}).
		MakeSymlink("/app/data", "/app/logs/data", nil)

	_ = template
}
```

## Install packages

Package-manager helpers map to the current Go builder surface.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		PipInstall([]string{"requests", "pandas", "numpy"}, nil).
		PipInstall([]string{"requests", "pandas", "numpy"}, &struct{ G bool }{G: false}).
		NpmInstall([]string{"express", "lodash"}, nil).
		NpmInstall([]string{"express", "lodash"}, &struct{ G bool }{G: true}).
		BunInstall([]string{"express", "lodash"}, nil).
		BunInstall([]string{"express", "lodash"}, &struct{ G bool }{G: true}).
		AptInstall([]string{"curl", "wget", "git"}, nil)

	_ = template
}
```

## Git operations

Use `GitClone(...)` to clone repositories during template build.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		GitClone("https://github.com/user/repo.git").
		GitClone("https://github.com/user/repo.git", "/app/repo").
		GitClone("https://github.com/user/repo.git", "/app/repo", &struct{ Branch string }{Branch: "main"}).
		GitClone("https://github.com/user/repo.git", "/app/repo", &struct{ Depth int }{Depth: 1})

	_ = template
}
```

## Environment variables

Use `SetEnvs(...)` to add environment variables to the template definition.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(nil).SetEnvs(map[string]string{
		"NODE_ENV": "production",
		"API_KEY":  "your-api-key",
		"DEBUG":    "true",
	})

	_ = template
}
```

## Run commands during build

Use `RunCmd(...)` for shell commands executed during template build.

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		RunCmd("apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl").
		RunCmd([]string{"apt-get update", "apt-get install -y curl", "curl --version"}).
		RunCmd("npm install", &struct{ User string }{User: "node"})

	_ = template
}
```

For base images, see [Base Image](/docs/template/base-image). For build execution and logging, see [Build](/docs/template/build) and [Logging](/docs/template/logging).
